race.name: American Birkebeiner
race.tagline: Ten thousand skiers charging through the Northwoods — America's largest and loudest cross-country ski race.

vitals.registration: Opens in spring; early-bird pricing available
vitals.aid_stations: Multiple stations with cookies, energy drink, water, warm broth

climate.description: Late February conditions in northern Wisconsin range from bitter cold to surprisingly mild. Temperatures typically -20°C to 0°C. The race has been shortened or rerouted due to both extreme cold and insufficient snow in recent years, reflecting climate variability.
challenges: Extreme cold in some years — wind chill below -30°C possible
Snow reliability declining — race has been shortened or altered in recent years
Flat light in overcast conditions makes trail reading difficult
Midwestern wind exposure on open sections

course.primary: Point-to-point through the Northwoods forest from Cable to Hayward on the American Birkebeiner Ski Trail. Rolling terrain with significant hills — the course is hillier than many expect. The famous finish on Main Street Hayward is one of skiing's great spectacles.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through hardwood and pine forest
course.grooming: High-quality machine grooming, well-maintained American Birkebeiner trail system
features: Iconic finish on Main Street Hayward with massive crowds
Offers both classic and skate distances on separate courses
The 'Birkie' trail system is groomed year-round for training
Rolling hills through Northwoods hardwood forest
Post-race festival in Hayward with live music and community celebration

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: North America's premier citizen ski race with unmatched community atmosphere. Low altitude and declining snow reliability are the main limiting factors. The Main Street Hayward finish is legendary.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 promotes from base TIER 2 — stays TIER 2 (cannot enter TIER 1)

history.summary: Founded in 1973 by Tony Wise, inspired by Norway's Birkebeinerrennet. The first race had 35 skiers; it has grown to over 10,000 participants, making it the largest cross-country ski race in North America. The race follows the American Birkebeiner Ski Trail through the Northwoods of Wisconsin. The Birkie has become a cultural institution in the Upper Midwest and a gateway event that introduces thousands of Americans to competitive skiing.
notable_facts: First race in 1973 had just 35 skiers — now North America's largest
The Main Street Hayward finish draws thousands of spectators
The Birkie trail system is maintained year-round and used for mountain biking in summer
Has been shortened or rerouted several times due to insufficient snow or extreme cold
